info.title: Agendor API
info.description:
# Introduction

Welcome to our API docs! Agendor is a CRM system made to streamline the
sales process of teams of inside and outside sales. With our API, you
can integrate your existing systems with Agendor and make your sales
team even more efficient. Check out some help articles our team has
written about integrating with our API (in Portuguese):

* [Do you have examples of how to use the Agendor API?](https://ajuda.agendor.com.br/integracoes/voces-tem-exemplos-de-como-utilizar-a-api-do-agendor)
* [Can I integrate Agendor to my current system?](https://ajuda.agendor.com.br/integracoes/o-agendor-pode-ser-integrado-ao-meu-sistema-atual)

## Structure

Our API is a RESTful JSON API, which means:

* The endpoints are structured around *resources*, identified by URIs;
* HTTP *verbs* are used to act differently on the same resource
and HTTP *status codes* are used to signal the result of an operation;
* We expect body parameters in JSON and also respond in JSON.

The body of succesful responses will have up to three main root level
properties:

* `data`: actual data of the resource you're working with, will always
be present;
* `meta`: metadata about the resource, for instance total size of a
collection of results;
* `links`: links to related resources, which can be used to navigate
through the API.

The failure responses will be signaled by HTTP status codes and also
have error details under the `errors` property in the response body.

## Authentication

<!-- ReDoc-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

To authenticate your requests, you need an API token. You can find
your token by logging into Agendor, and going to
[Menu > Integrações](https://web.agendor.com.br/sistema/integracoes).
This token is like your password, so keep it secret!

Add this token to the `Authorization` header of your requests, using
the `Token` scheme, for example, in cURL:

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: Token your-token-here' https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/users/me
```

In PHP, you might do:

```php
$curl = curl_init();

curl_setopt($curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array("Authorization: Token your-token-here"));
curl_setopt($curl, CURLOPT_URL, "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/users/me");

$response = curl_exec($curl);
```

In case of failed authentication, the API will respond with a `401`
status code and the following body:

```json
{
  "errors": [
    "Token could not be authenticated"
  ]
}
```

If you're getting this error, you might be using an invalid token or
your headers are not set up properly. Check if your Agendor account and
user are active and if you have included your token in the `Authorization`
header, preceded by the word `Token`.

## Pagination

In the endpoints where you might `GET` a collection of resources, they
will be paginated. By default, `10` items are returned per page, but
you can increase the page size up to `100`.

To navigate through the results, use the `page` parameter, starting
from page `1`. The total count of results will be included in the
`meta` property of the response and links for the following and
preceding pages will be  included under `links`:

```bash
GET https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=2

{
  "data": [...
  ],
  "meta": {
    "totalCount": 100
  },
  "links": {
    "self": "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=2",
    "prev": "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=1",
    "next": "https://api.agendor.com.br/v3/organizations?page=3"
  }
}
```

get.description: List and search tasks of a person.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page of results to fetch.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: finishedDateGt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks that were **finished after** this date.

parameters.name: finishedDateLt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks that were **finished before** this date.

parameters.name: dueDateGt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks **due after** this date.

parameters.name: dueDateLt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks **due before** this date.

parameters.name: updatedDateGt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks that were **updated after** this date.

parameters.name: updatedDateLt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks that were **updated before** this date.

parameters.name: createdDateGt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks that were **created after** this date.

parameters.name: createdDateLt
parameters.description: Use to search for tasks that were **created before** this date.

parameters.name: metricId
parameters.description: Metric ID.

parameters.name: finishedEq
parameters.description: Use to search for finished or unfinished tasks.

post.description: Create a new task related to this person.

put.description: Update an existing task of this person.

get.description: List deals of this person.

parameters.name: withCustomFields
parameters.description:
Return custom fields of deals.
                 Use this param to include them in the response.
                 Example: /people/{person_id}/deals?withCustomFields=true

post.description: Create a new deal for person.

get.description: List and search for people.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page of results to fetch.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name **prefix**.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Category ID.

parameters.name: leadOrigin
parameters.description: Lead origin ID.

parameters.name: products
parameters.description: Array of product IDS.

parameters.name: author
parameters.description: Author user ID.

parameters.name: userOwner
parameters.description: Owner user ID.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Role prefix.

parameters.name: cpf
parameters.description: Document number (CPF) **prefix**.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description: Organization ID.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Address state abbreviation.

parameters.name: cityName
parameters.description: City name **prefix**.

parameters.name: district
parameters.description: District name **prefix**.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Contact email **prefix**.

parameters.name: work_phone
parameters.description: Work phone number.

parameters.name: mobile_phone
parameters.description: Mobile phone number.

parameters.name: fax_phone
parameters.description: Fax number.

parameters.name: whatsapp
parameters.description: WhatsApp phone number.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Use to search in any phone field (work, mobile, fax and WhatsApp).

parameters.name: createdAtGt
parameters.description: Use to search for people that were **created after** this date.

parameters.name: createdAtLt
parameters.description: Use to search for people that were **created before** this date.

parameters.name: updatedAtGt
parameters.description: Use to search for people that were **updated after** this date.

parameters.name: updatedAtLt
parameters.description: Use to search for people that were **updated before** this date.

parameters.name: withCustomFields
parameters.description:
Return custom fields of people. Use this param to include them in the response.
           Example: /people?withCustomFields=true

get.description: Get an specific person by ID.

parameters.name: withCustomFields
parameters.description:
Return custom fields of people. Use this param to include them in the response.
             Example: /people/{id}?withCustomFields=true

put.description: Update a specific person by ID.

post.description:
Search for a person by cpf or email.

If found, update it, otherwise, create a new one.

This endpoint is very useful if you want to avoid verifying for
duplicates before creating a new person.

get.description:
List deals stages changes in a time range.
            This allows to track everytime a deal had a movement between stages
            of a funnel or when it was won or lost.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page of results to fetch.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
Use to search for stages or status created and updated
                 **since** the given date. Example: 2023-10-23T00:00:00Z.
                 When not provided, default value is 7 days ago.

parameters.name: days_interval
parameters.description:
Amount of days **after since** to delimit the filter range.
                 Default value is 7. Max value is 30.
